generic_reference.text: Under 1-2-5h, an EXAMPLE is provided but the preceding paragraph does not include specific prescribed phraseology. How must a controller treat the example text?
The note to 10-2-4 describes volcanic ash damage to high-bypass turbofan engines. Which part of the paragraph does this note most directly constrain?

Under 13-1-3, trial planning is used to evaluate the feasibility of removing a flight direction constraint. What does the paragraph identify as an example of an inappropriate constraint on flight direction?
Which transmission best follows the paragraph's guidance for describing a mower to the left of Runway 27?
Complete the rule: Before you provide radar service, establish and maintain _____ of the aircraft involved.
Under 6-5-2, GPS-equipped aircraft tracking a waypoint receive 'to' data rather than 'from' data. What operational adjustment does the paragraph require for crossing restrictions?
Under 8-3-2, how does the longitudinal separation rule for same tracks differ from the rule for crossing tracks?
Under 8-7-1, the general oceanic procedures in Chapter 8 apply to the North Atlantic ICAO Region with _____ prescribed by this section.
Under 8-7-2, vertical separation in the North Atlantic ICAO Region is provided in accordance with Chapter _____, Section 8, unless this section prescribes otherwise.
Under 8-7-3, longitudinal separation in the North Atlantic ICAO Region is provided in accordance with Chapter 8, Section _____, unless this section prescribes otherwise.
Under 8-7-4, lateral separation in the North Atlantic ICAO Region is provided in accordance with Chapter 8, Section _____, unless this section prescribes otherwise.
Under 8-8-2, vertical separation in the Caribbean ICAO Region is provided in accordance with Chapter _____, Section 8, unless this section prescribes otherwise.
Under 9-3-1, are Alert Areas and Controlled Firing Areas subject to the separation procedures of this section?
Under 9-3-1, what are the five conditions under which the section's separation procedures do NOT apply to aircraft near SUA, ATCAAs, or stationary ALTRVs?
Under 9-3-2, for stationary ALTRVs and TFRs protecting aircraft hazard areas (AHA) during space launch or reentry, what is the rule about nonparticipating aircraft?
Under 9-4-2, a controller tells a fuel-dumping aircraft 'Fly heading 270 for traffic.' The dump is not for emergency reasons. Is this an authorized use of the rule?
Under 9-7-3, what type of airspace does this paragraph apply to for parachute operations?

location_dependent.text: Under 1-1-1, what are controllers required to do if they encounter situations not covered by this order?
Under 1-1-1, which provisions of this order are controllers required to be familiar with?
Under 1-1-10, which of the following correctly describes the relationship between an LOA and FAA Order JO 7110.65 minima?
Under 1-1-10, which procedures or minima trigger the requirement for a letter of agreement?

Under 1-1-11, a waiver request involves military operations. The facility supervisor obtains FAA regional approval first, then notifies the military headquarters after the waiver is approved. Is this the correct sequence?
Under the Terminal note in 1-1-11, who approves USAF procedures or minima differing from the order and involving military aircraft only?
Under 1-1-12, what are all ATO employees expected to do beyond simply maintaining safety in the NAS?
Under 1-1-12, which of the following is NOT listed as a way to obtain additional ATO SMS information?
Under 1-1-13, a facility is physically located in the Eastern region but its service area office is in the Central region. The facility needs to contact a non-ATO FAA office. Which region is correct?
Under 1-1-13, when contacting a non-ATO FAA regional office, which region determines the correct contact?
Under 1-1-13, which examples are given of FAA regional office organizations that are not part of the Air Traffic Organization?
Under 1-1-14a, which of the following is listed as a recipient of this order distribution?
Under 1-1-2, which of the following is true about who this order applies to?
Under 1-1-3, the order is available on the FAA's Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the FAA _____ website.
A user claims this order can be found on the MyFAA employee portal but not on any public website. Is that correct under 1-1-3?
Under 1-1-3, which two websites make this order available online?
Under 1-1-4, when JO 7110.65BB was issued, what happened to JO 7110.65AA changes?
Under 1-1-4, when JO 7110.65BB was issued, the changes to JO 7110.65AA remained in effect as standalone guidance until individually reviewed and re-issued.
Under 1-1-5, for how long is it advisable to retain the Explanation of Changes pages?
Under 1-1-5, what is the difference between where changes are identified and where the full order text lives?
Complete: Under 1-1-6, the cutoff date for completion is the deadline for a proposed change to be fully _____ and signed.
Under 1-1-6, this order and its changes are scheduled to be published to coincide with what dates?
Under 1-1-6, what must a change initiator do to meet the publication effective date?
Under 1-1-7, if a military facility has not received the order or changes at least 30 days before the effective date, what must it do and which table provides the contact information?
Under 1-1-7, what is the common time threshold that applies to website availability, FAA facility notification, and military facility notification?
Under 1-1-8, which FAA order contains additional details on the submission process and AJV-P processing responsibilities?
Under 1-1-8d, when will procedural changes be made to this order?
Under 1-1-9, how does the routing for DoD personnel requests differ from the routing for non-FAA/DoD requests?
Under 1-1-9a2, what two-outcome determination must the Service Area Director make for an AJT field interpretation request?
Complete: Under 1-2-1i, "approved separation" means separation in accordance with the applicable _____ in this order.
Under 1-2-1l, course, bearing, azimuth, heading, and wind direction information must always be _____ unless specifically stated otherwise.
Under 1-2-1k, when is "miles" interpreted as statute miles rather than nautical miles?
Under 1-2-1m, how are time checks given, and how does this differ from the standard time convention for ATC operational activities?
Under 1-2-1m, when does the change to the next minute occur for ATC operational time?
Under 1-2-1n, a procedure references "the runway." The facility has both a concrete runway and a helipad with a takeoff course. Does the procedure apply to the helipad unless otherwise specified?
Complete: Under 1-2-2a, same courses have an angular difference of less than _____ degrees.
Complete: Under 1-2-2c, opposite/reciprocal courses have an angular difference greater than _____ degrees through 180 degrees inclusive.
Under 1-2-2, two aircraft have intersecting protected airspaces with an angular difference of exactly 135 degrees. How are they classified?
Under 1-2-2, what condition must also be met beyond angular difference for courses to be classified as same or opposite/reciprocal?
Complete: Under 1-2-5c, bold vertical lines in the margin of changed pages indicate the location of _____ revisions to the order.

stems: Under 2-7-3, high barometric pressure procedures are triggered when the barometric pressure is greater than _____ inches mercury.
Under 2-7-3, when high barometric pressure procedures are in effect, en route/arrival controllers must advise pilots to leave the altimeter set to _____ until reaching the final approach fix.
For a T-class VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN, the normal usable altitude band is _____ with a usable radius distance of _____ miles.
For an L-class VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN, the normal usable radius distance is 40 miles. What is the altitude band?
In terminal arrival operations under 6-1-5a, which of the following correctly states the separation for a Category E, F, or G aircraft behind a Category C aircraft?

Under 6-1-5c en route arrivals to a parallel runway separated by less than 2,500 feet (or a crossing runway with crossing projected flight paths), what separation applies to a small aircraft behind a B757?
Under 6-5-4b, when a course change is 16 through 90 degrees, what overflown-side protection applies at FL180 to FL230 inclusive?
Under 6-5-4c, when a course change is 91 through 180 degrees at FL180 to FL230 inclusive, what overflown-side protection applies?
Under 6-7-5b (EN ROUTE), what timed-approach wake interval applies to a large aircraft behind a super?
Under 6-7-5c1 (TERMINAL), what timed-approach wake interval applies to a Category E, F, or G aircraft behind a Category A?
Under 8-4-3, after the reduced-width segment, protected airspace increases on a _____ degree angle from the route centerline, measured at the NAVAID.
Under 8-4-3, below FL 240, reduce the width of protected airspace to _____ miles on each side of the route centerline to a distance of 57.14 miles from the NAVAID.

near_duplicates.left: For a T-class VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN, the normal usable altitude band is _____ with a usable radius distance of _____ miles.
near_duplicates.right: For an L-class VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN, the normal usable radius distance is 40 miles. What is the altitude band?

near_duplicates.left: Under 6-5-4b, when a course change is 16 through 90 degrees, what overflown-side protection applies at FL180 to FL230 inclusive?
near_duplicates.right: Under 6-5-4c, when a course change is 91 through 180 degrees at FL180 to FL230 inclusive, what overflown-side protection applies?

near_duplicates.left: If deviations from the final approach course are observed after initial course interception and the aircraft is INSIDE the approach gate, what should the controller do?
near_duplicates.right: If deviations from the final approach course are observed after initial course interception and the aircraft is OUTSIDE the approach gate, what should the controller do?

near_duplicates.left: When an unpublished fix is located beyond 45 NM from the NAVAID generating the off-course radial, the minimum divergence angle must increase at what rate?
near_duplicates.right: When an unpublished fix is located over 30 NM from the NAVAID generating the off-course radial, the minimum divergence angle must increase at _____ up to 45 NM.

near_duplicates.left: When an unpublished fix is located over 30 NM from the NAVAID generating the off-course radial, the minimum divergence angle must increase at _____ up to 45 NM.
near_duplicates.right: An unpublished fix is located 40 NM from the NAVAID generating the off-course radial. What is the minimum divergence angle?

uncovered_essential_statements.statement: Increased Separation Required ELP . . . . . . .
Required Navigation Performance TDWR . . . . .
Participating units maintain specific procedures for conducting these operations.
The recommended escape maneuver is to reverse course and begin a descent (if terrain permits).

Consider an aircraft to be overdue and initiate the procedures stated in this section to issue an ALNOT when neither communications nor radar contact can be established and 30 minutes have passed since:
A VFR or IFR aircraft arriving at an airport not served by an air traffic control tower or flight service station fails to cancel a flight plan after receiving instructions on how to cancel.
If you have reason to believe that an aircraft is overdue prior to 30 minutes, take the appropriate action immediately.

Consider an aircraft to be in an emergency status and initiate ALNOT procedures in this section immediately when there is an abnormal simultaneous loss of radar and communications with an IFR aircraft or VFR/SVFR aircraft receiving flight following services.
The ARTCC in whose area the aircraft is reported as overdue, missing or lost will make these determinations and takes any subsequent action required.
If you have reason to believe that an aircraft is overdue prior to 30 minutes, take the appropriate action immediately.
The center in whose area the aircraft is first unreported or overdue will make these determinations and takes any subsequent action required.

TERMINAL When an aircraft is considered to be in emergency status that may require SAR procedures, or an IFR aircraft is overdue, the terminal facility must alert the appropriate ARTCC and forward the following information, as available:
Flight plan, including color of aircraft, if known.
Position of other aircraft near aircraft’s route of flight, when requested.
Non−FSS ATC facilities that receive telephone calls or other inquiries regarding these flights must refer these calls and inquiries to the appropriate FSS.
EN ROUTE When an aircraft is considered to be in emergency status or an IFR aircraft is overdue, the ARTCC must alert the RCC and forward the following information, as available:
Flight plan, including color of aircraft, if known.
Position of other aircraft near aircraft’s route of flight, when requested.
Non−FSS ATC facilities that receive telephone calls or other inquiries regarding these flights must refer these calls and inquiries to the appropriate FSS.

In addition to routing to the regional office operations center for the area in which the facility is located, issue an ALNOT to all centers and Area B circuits, generally 50 miles on either side of the route of flight from the last reported position to destination.
An ALNOT must be issued before the RCC can begin search and rescue procedures.
Retain the alert in an active status, and immediately notify the originator of subsequent contact, until cancellation is received.
When information is obtained not previously contained in the ALNOT, issue an amended ALNOT to update information that will assist the SAR providers.
EN ROUTE Transfer responsibility for further search to the RCC when one of the following occurs:
Thirty minutes have elapsed after the estimated aircraft fuel exhaustion time.
The aircraft has not been located within one hour after ALNOT issuance.

To assist the RCC and SAR Teams in the conduct of the SAR mission, provide the most accurate latitude and longitude available to the FAA using en route and terminal radar sensor data near the aircraft’s last known position.
If necessary to prevent an undue delay, utilize any available method to determine the initial latitude and longitude.
If available, solicit the assistance of other aircraft known to be operating near the aircraft in distress.
When directed by the RCC, cancel the ALNOT when the aircraft is located or the search is abandoned.
IFR traffic which could be affected by an overdue or unreported aircraft must be restricted or suspended unless radar separation is used.
The facility responsible must restrict or suspend IFR traffic for a period of 30 minutes following the applicable time listed in subparagraphs a through e:
The release time and, if issued, the clearance void time.

At nontower or non−FSS locations, request the airport management to light all runway lights, approach lights, and all other required airport lighting systems for at least 30 minutes before the ETA of the unreported aircraft until the aircraft has been located or for 30 minutes after its fuel supply is estimated to be exhausted.

Operate runway lights, approach lights, and all other required airport lighting systems for at least 30 minutes before the ETA of the unreported aircraft until the aircraft has been located or for 30 minutes after its fuel supply is estimated to be exhausted.
After the 30−minute traffic suspension period has expired, resume normal air traffic control if the operators or pilots of other aircraft concur.
This concurrence must be maintained for a period of 30 minutes after the suspension period has expired.
Take the following actions, as appropriate, if two-way radio communications are lost with an aircraft:
When an IFR aircraft experiences two-way radio communications failure, air traffic control is based on anticipated pilot actions.
Provide the aircraft call sign, approximate location, and contact instructions.
In order to utilize the SELCAL system, the SELCAL code for the subject aircraft must be known.
If the SELCAL code is not contained in the remarks section of the flight plan, contact the pertinent air carrier dispatch office to determine the code.

Then contact San Francisco Radio (for aircraft over the Pacific, U.S. or Mexico) or New York Radio (for aircraft over the Atlantic, Gulf of America, or Caribbean) and provide the aircraft call sign, SELCAL code, approximate location, and contact instructions.
Some UHF equipped aircraft have VHF navigation equipment and can receive 121.5 MHz.
(Action) OBSERVED, (additional instructions/information if necessary).
Broadcast a clearance for the aircraft to proceed to its filed alternate airport at the MEA if the aircraft operator concurs.
TERMINAL When you receive information that an emergency landing will be made with explosive cargo aboard, inform the pilot of the safest or least congested airport areas.
The appropriate military agencies, when requested by the pilot.

In the event of a debris−generating space launch or reentry vehicle mishap, issue an alert broadcast to all affected aircraft informing them of the mishap, and, if known, the approximate location of the debris fall area.
If a debris response area (DRA) has been activated, issue the approximate location of the response area instead.
When workload permits, reissue the alert broadcast approximately every 15 minutes.
When advised that falling debris is no longer a factor, or the DRA has been deactivated, issue a broadcast to advise all aircraft of this information.
Issue instructions and/or clearances to prevent aircraft from entering the debris response area, unless a higher priority duty already exists.
When there is concern about the safety of an aircraft or its occupants, an INCERFA exists:

When communication from an aircraft has not been received within 30 minutes after the time a communication should have been received or after the time an unsuccessful attempt to establish communication with such aircraft was first made, whichever is earlier; or
When an aircraft fails to arrive within 30 minutes after the time of arrival last estimated by the pilot or by the ATC units, whichever is later.
When there is apprehension about the safety of an aircraft and its occupants, an ALERFA exists:

Following the uncertainty phase when subsequent attempts to establish communications with the aircraft, or inquiries to other relevant sources have failed to reveal any information about the aircraft; or
When information has been received which indicates that the operating efficiency of the aircraft has been impaired but not to the extent that a forced landing is likely; or

When communication from an aircraft has not been received within 60 minutes after the time a communication should have been received or after the time an unsuccessful attempt to establish communication with such aircraft was first made, whichever is earlier.
When there is reasonable certainty that the aircraft and its occupants are threatened by grave and imminent danger, a DETRESFA exists:
Following the alert phase when further attempts to establish communications with the aircraft and more widespread inquiries are unsuccessful; or
When the fuel on board is considered to be exhausted or to be insufficient for the aircraft to reach safety; or
When information is received which indicates that the operating efficiency of the aircraft has been impaired to the extent that a forced landing is likely; or
When information is received or it is reasonably certain that the aircraft is about to make or has made a forced landing.
When alerting service is required, the responsibility for coordinating such service must, unless otherwise established by letter of agreement, rest with the facility serving the FIR or CTA:
Which the aircraft was about to enter if the last air-ground contact was established at or close to the boundary; or
Within which the point of destination is located if the aircraft:

If an aircraft over water requests weather, sea conditions, ditching information, and/or assistance from surface vessels, or if the controller feels that this information may be necessary for aircraft safety, it should be requested from the RCC.
Also, an appropriate AMVER SURPIC should be asked for if requested by the aircraft or deemed beneficial by control personnel.
In all cases of aircraft ditching, the airspace required for SAR operations must be determined by the RCC.
The ACC must block that airspace until the RCC advises the airspace is no longer required.
An International Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) must be issued describing the airspace affected.
The following actions will be taken in the event an aircraft must make an emergency descent:
(a) Issue a clearance to the requested altitude if approved separation can be provided.
(b) Advise the aircraft of the traffic, and request its intentions if traffic prevents an unrestricted descent.
(a) Advise other aircraft of the emergency descent.
(b) Advise other aircraft when the emergency descent is complete.

When appropriate, broadcast by ATC communications, by radio navigation aids, and/or through aeronautical communication stations/services an emergency message to all aircraft in the vicinity of the descending aircraft.
If traffic conditions permit, provide traffic information to the affected aircraft.
